title: Regina officer becomes first Black woman in police force history

paragraphs.english: REGINA — When Const. Desiree Ates joined the Regina Police Service earlier this year, she made history as the force’s first Black female officer — something she didn’t realize until she was already on the path to policing.
paragraphs.pidgin: REGINA — When Const. Desiree Ates join di Regina Police Service earlier dis year, im make history as di force’s first Black female officer — sometin im didn’t realize until im bin dey don already on di path to policing.

paragraphs.english: Joining a police force had always been in the back of Ates’ mind, but she never knew the impact it could have.
paragraphs.pidgin: Joining a police force bin get always dey for di back of Ates’ mind, but im never know di impact e fit get.

paragraphs.english: Born on a U.S. Air Force base in Germany, Ates comes from a diverse background. Her father, who is Black, is from Georgia, U.S., and served in the military. Her mother is from Canoe Lake Cree First Nation in Saskatchewan.
paragraphs.pidgin: Born on a U.S. Air Force base for Germany, Ates comes from a diverse background. Im father, who na Black, na from Georgia, U.S., and served for di military. Im mother na from Canoe Lake Cree First Nation for Saskatchewan.

paragraphs.english: Ates later moved to Regina, attending high school and university in the Queen City. When deciding on her future, she said she first wanted to explore and learn more about herself.
paragraphs.pidgin: Ates later moved to Regina, attending high school and university for di Queen City. When deciding on im future, im tok im first wanted to explore and learn more about imself.

paragraphs.english: “I travelled a lot. And so I gained a lot of life experience through that. And then I decided that, Ok, I'm in my 30s now. Now it's time to find a career.”
paragraphs.pidgin: “I travelled a lot. And so I gained plenti life experience through dat. And then I decide dat, Ok, I'm for my 30s now. Now e im time to find a career.”

paragraphs.english: Unsure about her next step, Ates travelled to a military base in Arizona to learn more about serving.
paragraphs.pidgin: Unsure about im next step, Ates travelled to a military base for Arizona to learn more about serving.

paragraphs.english: “I knew that there was part of… an agency that I wanted to be a part of. But I just didn't know what that really looked like, if it was going to be the military route or if it was going to be a police agency.”
paragraphs.pidgin: “I know dat e dey part of… an agency dat I wanted to be a part of. But I just no know wetin dat true true looked like, if e bin dey going to be di military route or if e bin dey going to be a police agency.”

paragraphs.english: Ates eventually enroled in the Saskatchewan Police College, unaware that the Regina Police Service had never had a Black female officer before.
paragraphs.pidgin: Ates eventually enroled for di Saskatchewan Police College, unaware dat di Regina Police Service bin get never bin get a Black female officer before.

paragraphs.english: “I had joked about it, just being like, ‘Oh, how great it would be to be the first Black female officer.’ Not knowing that that was an actual reality.”
paragraphs.pidgin: “I bin $2 about e, just being like, ‘Oh, how great e go be to be di first Black female officer.’ No knowing dat dat bin an actual reality.”

paragraphs.english: Ates said learning the distinction was both surprising and understandable.
paragraphs.pidgin: Ates tok learning di distinction bin both surprising and understandable.

paragraphs.english: “Policing is a career that not everyone wants to get into. It's a very niche career, and you have to be a certain type of person to get into policing. And not everyone enjoys that.”
paragraphs.pidgin: “Policing na a career dat no everybody wants to get into. E im a very niche career, and you get to be a certain type of person to get into policing. And no everybody enjoys dat.”

paragraphs.english: She graduated from police college in December 2025 and recalled the pride her parents felt at the milestone.
paragraphs.pidgin: Im graduated from police college for December 2025 and recalled di pride im parents feel for di milestone.

paragraphs.english: “It was definitely a lot of proud moments that my parents have had over the last little bit.”
paragraphs.pidgin: “e bin na definitely plenti proud moments dat my parents get bin get over di last small small bit.”

paragraphs.english: As the police service’s first Black female officer, Ates said representation is extremely important, noting that seeing someone who looks like you in uniform can encourage others to consider the career.
paragraphs.pidgin: As di police service’s first Black female officer, Ates tok representation na extremely important, noting dat seeing somebody who looks like you for uniform fit encourage others to consider di career.

paragraphs.english: Ates believes Canadian police forces need to reflect the diversity of the communities they serve.
paragraphs.pidgin: Ates believes Canadian police forces need to reflect di diversity of di communities dem serve.

paragraphs.english: “We need different types of officers throughout the agency. And I think it's one of those things that maybe somebody's not as strong in communication, where I feel like I can maybe step up and have those conversations.”
paragraphs.pidgin: “We need different types of officers throughout di agency. And I think e im one of dem ones things dat maybe somebody im no as strong for communication, where I feel like I fit maybe step up and get dem ones conversations.”

paragraphs.english: She also said people sometimes feel more comfortable speaking with officers they can relate to.
paragraphs.pidgin: Im also tok pipul sometimes feel more comfortable speaking wit officers dem fit relate to.

paragraphs.english: “When somebody looks a little bit different or maybe looks like you, it's like, oh, yes, I see that, and I want to speak to them.”
paragraphs.pidgin: “When somebody looks a small small bit different or maybe looks like you, e im like, oh, yes, I see dat, and I want to talk to dem.”

paragraphs.english: However, she acknowledged that experiences can vary.
paragraphs.pidgin: but, im acknowledged dat experiences fit vary.

paragraphs.english: “There are times where it is a benefit and that people do gravitate towards me, but then there are other times when they don't, also. And that's just part of it.”
paragraphs.pidgin: “e dey times where e na a benefit and dat pipul do gravitate towards me, but then e dey other times when dem don't, also. And dat im just part of e.”

paragraphs.english: Over the last few years, the Regina Police Service has identified a lack of representation within the force and has been working on strategies to become more inclusive.
paragraphs.pidgin: Over di last small small years, di Regina Police Service don identified a lack of representation inside di force and don dey working on strategies to turn more inclusive.

paragraphs.english: Ates said that effort is part of adapting to a changing community.
paragraphs.pidgin: Ates tok tok effort na part of adapting to a changing community.

paragraphs.english: “The more diverse our community gets, the more diverse the police agency, I think, should reflect that community that they're serving and protecting.”
paragraphs.pidgin: “Di more diverse our community gets, di more diverse di police agency, I think, suppose to reflect dat community dat dem're serving and protecting.”

paragraphs.english: Having joined the force in January, Ates plans to continue working as a patrol officer while exploring other opportunities within the RPS.
paragraphs.pidgin: Having join di force for January, Ates plans to continue working as a patrol officer while exploring other opportunities inside di RPS.

paragraphs.english: “I would love to do SWAT. I would love to do K-9. Forensics is really interesting to me. Being a spotter on the airplane would be really cool. So a school resource officer would be awesome.”
paragraphs.pidgin: “I go love to do SWAT. I go love to do K-9. Forensics na true true interesting to me. Being a spotter on di airplane go be true true ogbonge. So a school resource officer go be ogbonge.”
